Off Calendar Aug-05-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jeffrey Wehr of Brayton Purcell (898-1555) for plaintiff and responding party
Todd Thacker of Jackson & Wallace (982.6300) for defendant and moving party

Argued and the Court took DEFENDANT PCC FLOW TECHNOLOGIES LP'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT under submission. (302/PJM/AY) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-18-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ROBERTSHAW CONTROLS COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. Defendant shifted the burden of production. Plaintiff has presented evidence of a triable issue of fact. Plaintiff's declaration is not excluded under D'Amico. Construing the declaration in favor of plaintiff as the court must do, the reasonable inference is that defendant's work disturbed asbestos in the ceiling. The fact that plaintiff did not see it happen goes to the persuasiveness of the evidence not its admissibility. Aye has sufficient expertise to offer the opinion stated. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/AY) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-18-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ROBERT E MCKEE, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Appearances:
Natalie Nuttall of McKenna Long & Aldridge (267-4054) for defendant and moving party
Jeffrey Wehr of Brayton Purcell (898-1555) for plaintiff and responding party

Argued and the Court modified its tentative ruling as follows: denied. Defendant failed to shift the burden of proof. Defendant did not obtain responses to special interrogatories. As a consequence, defendant is unable to demonstrate that plaintiff cannot prove exposure to asbestos by defendant's employees. The Mooney and Aye declarations also create a triable issue of fact. The objection to the Aye declaration is overruled. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/AY) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
